# host_mac_login_item (Resource)

Manages one macOS Login Item by application bundle path.

Use this resource for GUI apps that should open at login. The provider uses macOS automation through `osascript`, adopts an existing login item with the same path, and removes the login item when the resource is destroyed.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_package_brew" "hammerspoon" {
  name         = "hammerspoon"
  package_type = "cask"
}

resource "host_mac_login_item" "hammerspoon" {
  path = host_package_brew.hammerspoon.app_path
}
```

## Import

Import an existing Login Item by application bundle path:

```shell
terraform import host_mac_login_item.hammerspoon /Applications/Hammerspoon.app
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `path` (String) Application bundle path to open at login. `~` is expanded to the provider `home_dir` and relative paths are resolved from the Terraform working directory.

### Optional

- `hidden` (Boolean) Whether macOS should hide the app when opening it at login. Defaults to false.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `path`.
- `name` (String) Login item name reported by macOS.
- `path_resolved` (String) Resolved absolute application bundle path.
